facts.text: Aspen grew from a silver mining camp founded in 1879, later named for the many aspen trees that blanket the valley.
The town’s first railroad, the Colorado Midland, reached Aspen in 1886, sparking a boom that rivaled the Denver and Rio Grande’s arrival the same year.
In 1946, the Aspen Skiing Company was founded, turning the mining town into a world‑renowned ski resort that hosted the 1950 FIS World Championships.
The Wheeler Opera House, opened in 1889 as part of the “Silver Circuit,” remains a historic venue where international performers once dazzled miners.
The Holden/Marolt Mining and Ranching Museum, housed in a 19th‑century barn, preserves the largest industrial complex of Pitkin County’s mining past.
John Denver, the celebrated singer, wrote two songs about Aspen after making the town his home.
